# outline-color:auto

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/search?q=outline-color%3Aauto%20CSS" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=outline-color" status="CSS UI 4" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Let the browser choose an accessible focus outline color.

## Representative syntax

```css
outline-color: auto;
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="outline-color:auto" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="The form demo keeps native controls and shows how CSS can shape the interaction surface without losing semantics." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use outline-color:auto in theme and state systems when you need to build color variations from semantic inputs instead of hand-tuning every state. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A warning banner can derive its background, border, and text color from one token and still adapt in dark mode.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Perceptual spaces help with ramps, but they do not replace contrast checks against real text sizes and backgrounds.</p>
</section>
</div>

## Source metadata

- Category: Forms, Controls, and Interactive UI
- Maturity / source: CSS UI 4
- Representative syntax: `outline-color: auto;`
- Position in category: 9 / 12
